text.title: Contextual Inquiry Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when applying contextual-inquiry; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Sessions held in a usability lab because field access denied; workflow observations are sanitised.
antipattern.fix: If field is impossible, switch to diary studies or remote contextual inquiry — do not call lab sessions contextual inquiry.

antipattern.symptom: Researcher suggests `have you tried Ctrl+F?` mid-task; workflow now contaminated.
antipattern.fix: Bracket suggestions; log them for later as 'researcher impulse' but never voice during the session.

antipattern.symptom: Notes summarise 'user struggled with login'; no verbatim quotes; affinity diagram becomes researcher fiction.
antipattern.root-cause: Notes taken at end of session from memory.
antipattern.fix: Real-time verbatim capture (paper or device); timestamped quotes only.
